tagline: Your personal atlas — track where you've been, learn as you go, dream about where's next.
why: Travel and curiosity are the same impulse. I wanted one place to hold both — somewhere to mark the countries you've lived, log what you found there, and wander the ones you haven't reached yet. Part travel diary, part interactive atlas.
how: Built on Next.js with a D3-powered SVG flat map and a WebGL 3D globe via react-globe.gl and Three.js. Country data, travel journal, and status tracking all live in Zustand and persist locally — nothing ever leaves your device.

features.title: Track your travels
features.description: Mark countries as Visited, Wishlist, or Blocked with a single click. A progress bar counts your tally against all 195 sovereign nations.

features.title: Travel journal
features.description: Per-country notes, star ratings, a would-return toggle, best time to visit, and a month-picker for logging visit dates.

features.title: Globe & flat map
features.description: Switch between a pannable Mercator map and an interactive 3D globe. Both views, same data.

features.title: Live advisories & flights
features.description: Blended US and Canada safety advisories on every country. A real aircraft rotates every minute with photo, route, and airport links.

features.title: Privacy-first
features.description: Everything stays in your browser. No account, no server, no tracking. Export or import your data anytime.
